fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Massachusetts Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Massachusetts - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to massachusetts, respiratory therapist compact massachusetts, massachusetts respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Massachusetts Board of Respiratory Care
board.mailing_address:
Board of Respiratory Care
250 Washington Street
Boston, MA 02108

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years on your birthday in even numbered years
quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) exam

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Massachusetts.

steps.title: Create an ePLACE Account
steps.description: Navigate to the ePLACE Portal at https://eplace.mass.gov/ and click 'Register a New Account.' Follow the instructions to create your personal account, including setting up a username and password.

steps.title: Start a New Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created ePLACE account. From your 'My Licenses & Permits' dashboard, select 'New License Application' and choose 'Respiratory Therapist' from the list of available professions.

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Fill out all sections of the online application form within the ePLACE Portal. Provide accurate and complete information regarding your personal details, educational background, and professional history.

steps.title: Upload Required Documents
steps.description: Digitally upload all necessary supporting documents directly to your ePLACE application. This typically includes official transcripts from your accredited respiratory therapy program and official verification of your NBRC credentials (CRT and RRT).

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $150.00 through the ePLACE Portal. Payment can typically be made using a credit card or electronic check.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application within the ePLACE Portal. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Massachusetts Board of Respiratory Care to prevent delays.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Massachusetts respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Massachusetts have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Massachusetts. To work in Massachusetts, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Massachusetts while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Massachusetts
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Massachusetts, use the ePLACE Portal to submit your application and all required supporting documentation.
